profile.background: Portsmouth City Council at-large (officially nonpartisan; D-aligned per civic network — Chair of Portsmouth Democratic Committee per VPAP; Sen. L. Louise Lucas + Sen. Mark Warner staff history; Hampton Roads Young Democrats VP; Run For Something 2020 endorsement). Term 1/1/2023-12/31/2026 — ON Nov 3, 2026 ballot (active 'Tillage for Portsmouth City Council' VPAP committee). Single-vector progressive rhetoric on safety / affordability / housing / education with zero quote-bearing positions on the 30 LOCAL-rubric cells.

vernon-tillage.notes_set: [2026-06-04 17:30 RESOLUTE refinement, confirming + finalizing 2026-06-04 15:50 disposition] Roster verified: still seated, running Nov 2026. Two-batch sweep located NO quote-bearing position on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage, family_child_sovereignty, christian_liberty, election_integrity, refuse_state_overreach, border_immigration, or self_defense cells. Soft signals on public_justice — campaign language pairs 'modernized public safety / task force' with social/economic 'root causes of crime and poverty' framing (WAVY candidate profile) but the quote in his own voice never reaches the explicit 'public health crisis' phrasing the rubric scores against → public_justice q3 left null per Doyle-precedent strict cite-only discipline. economic_stewardship q2 null — FY27 budget held tax rate at $1.24/$100 (Apr 2026 hearings) but Tillage's individual roll-call position not isolated in public sources; he campaigns on subsidies/credits framing rather than restraint. Portsmouth named on May 2025 DHS sanctuary-jurisdictions list (later removed per WTKR) but no Tillage statement on the listing — border_immigration q2 stays null. Not seated for the 1/14/2020 4-3 Second Amendment Constitutional City vote (sworn 3 years later) — no Tillage 2A roll-call to score. Confidence promoted to evidence_local (from prior roster_verified) on the strength of two complete sweeps + comprehensive sources documenting the absence of public positions. Recommend revisiting after the 2026 Portsmouth FY27 final-budget roll-call publication, any 2026 Council action on Pride / sanctuary / abortion / firearms / CRT-SOGI, and the Nov 2026 election.

profile.background: Portsmouth City Council at-large (officially nonpartisan; ran as Independent — cleanest 'truly Independent' of the 2024 cohort). Term 1/1/2025-12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. Endodontist + aerospace engineer + distillery owner; Catholic parochial elementary school. Four-pillar campaign frame: Crime / Education / Infrastructure / Economic Development with effectively zero culture-war content. One identifiable contrarian council vote (sole NO on 3/25/2025 6-1 VA §65.2-402(B) workers'-comp presumption for Portsmouth EMS) is ambiguous in motive.

yolanda-thomas.notes_set: [2026-06-04 17:30 RESOLUTE refinement, validating + extending 2026-06-04 15:50 disposition] Roster verified: still seated, term to Dec 2028. Spring 2026 FY26-cycle vote located that flips one cell from the prior all-null disposition: Thomas was 1 of 2 NO votes on the Portsmouth FY26 school-budget portion, stating from the dais 'From the very beginning, my request was to fully provide the ask for the schools. We did not come to that conclusion, so today I will be voting no on the budget for the schools.' (13News Now, May 2026). The vote was AGAINST under-funding (i.e., she demanded MORE school spending), which is anti-fiscal-restraint and counter to the rubric's deficit-spending-opposition framing → economic_stewardship q2 = FALSE (cited). Remaining 29 LOCAL cells stay null on a thorough two-batch sweep: NO QUOTE-BEARING POSITIONS on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage, family_child_sovereignty (her 2020-2024 PPS School Board platform centered on Dual Language Immersion / CTE / COVID protocols — process-and-program language; no parental-rights, choice/voucher, or curriculum-restriction position located), christian_liberty (no profession of Christ from the dais; no public-square Christian symbols/prayer/Sabbath advocacy; AI-generated 'St. Mark Missionary Baptist Church' affiliation could not be verified and is not used as evidence), election_integrity (she herself was BENEFICIARY of a 21-vote-margin recount in 2024 that confirmed her seat — neutral data point), public_justice q0 (single-vector rhetoric on 'first responders' / 'community precincts and social services outreach' — Doyle-precedent NULL), public_justice q3 (pairs police WITH social-services outreach but no 'public health crisis' quote in her own voice → null per strict discipline), refuse_state_overreach (no COVID/ESG/federalism statements), border_immigration q1/q2 (no Thomas statement on Portsmouth's May 2025 DHS sanctuary listing or Sheriff Moore's WSO/287(g) cancellation), self_defense (not seated for 2020 2A vote; no subsequent firearms-ordinance position located). Counter-archetype context: the FY26 school-budget NO vote confirms left-progressive priors (she voted NO because the increase was too SMALL, not too large) — no off-archetype heterodoxy located. Confidence promoted to evidence_local (from prior roster_verified) on the strength of two complete sweeps + the cited FY26 vote. Recommend revisiting after FY27 budget cycle, any 2026 Pride / sanctuary / abortion / firearms / CRT-SOGI council action, and her Nov 2028 re-election.

profile.background: Suffolk City Council representing Whaleyville Borough (officially nonpartisan). Took office Jan 2021 after defeating incumbent Curtis Milteer Sr. (Nov 2020 3-way race); re-elected Nov 2024 over Jason Wooldridge ~56-60%; current term Jan 2025-Dec 2028 (NOT on Nov 2026 ballot). Self-described 'self-made entrepreneur' / landscaping-company owner / known locally as 'The Turkey Man' for community turkey giveaways. After taking office in 2021, lost a $157,000 city landscaping contract due to council conflict-of-interest rules (WAVY).

profile.religion: Baptist (deacon, East End Baptist Church, Suffolk VA)
profile.background: 30-year Suffolk City Council incumbent (Nansemond Borough 1996-2014; Cypress Borough 2014-present). Vice Mayor 2002-2006 and 2015-2022. Current term expires Dec 31, 2026 — ON Nov 2026 ballot. Retired General Foreman at Newport News Shipbuilding & Drydock; U.S. Navy veteran; Deacon at East End Baptist Church, Suffolk. Most recent election Nov 2022 Cypress: Bennett 3,351 vs Wallace Johnson 1,674 (~67% margin). Suffolk Council is officially nonpartisan; Bennett reads as moderate/center-right pragmatist by record (motioned 2020 Constitutional/2A resolution + pushed for deeper FY27 tax cut) but no formal partisan endorsement located → party set I.

leroy-bennett.notes_set: [2026-06-04 17:30 RESOLUTE refinement, FRESH research] HETERODOXY-ADJACENT RECORD on Suffolk Council. Roster verified: 30-year continuous tenure (1996-present); current Cypress term to Dec 2026; running Nov 2026. Party fix Unknown→I (no partisan endorsement located; nonpartisan office; record reads moderate/center-right pragmatist). Religion: confirmed public Deacon at East End Baptist Church Suffolk — IDENTITY data point (does NOT meet the Bell precedent for christian_liberty q0 affirmative-policy-advocacy from the dais → q0 null per discipline). KEY VOTES: (1) Jan 15, 2020 Suffolk Council Constitutional City resolution — Vice Mayor Bennett MADE THE MOTION to adopt, seconded by Fawcett; passed unanimously. Suffolk pointedly declined the 'Sanctuary' label and used the broader 'Constitutional City' framing affirming all constitutional rights; the resolution was a direct local response to Virginia's then-pending firearms restrictions (red-flag, mag limits, AWB) → refuse_state_overreach q0 TRUE (asserted local constitutional control vs unconstitutional state firearms restrictions), refuse_state_overreach q1 TRUE (declined state strings compelling unconstitutional local policy by motioning preemptive constitutional defense), self_defense q1 TRUE (motioned the 2020 Constitutional resolution specifically opposing red-flag / mag limits / AWB / registries; Suffolk News-Herald + The New Journal & Guide). (2) FY2025-26 budget May 2025 — 7-1 approved; held real estate rate at $1.07; Bennett expressed concerns about the effective 4.71% increase from assessments, said 'we cannot control the assessment we can control the rate'. (3) FY2026-27 budget May 2026 — unanimous; 1¢ cut to $1.06; Bennett said the 1¢ cut 'was not enough' → economic_stewardship q2 TRUE (pushed for DEEPER rate cuts beyond the consensus 1¢; explicit anti-spending restraint posture beyond routine budget approval). NULL on the remaining 26 cells: NO QUOTE-BEARING POSITIONS on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage (Baptist deacon is identity not policy), family_child_sovereignty (council role; no school-choice/parental-notification/CRT-SOGI statement located), christian_liberty q0/q2/q3/q4 (Bell precedent — identity not policy), election_integrity (local council; no election-admin statement), public_justice q0 (no affirmative pro-police speech located beyond council's general LE budgeting → Doyle-precedent NULL), public_justice q1-q4, refuse_state_overreach q2-q4 (no COVID/ESG/subsidiarity speech located), border_immigration q1/q2 (Suffolk not on DHS sanctuary list as of May 2025 search; no Bennett ICE-cooperation statement; q2 null), self_defense q0 (no constitutional-carry statement). Counter-archetype context: 30-year deep-tenure Baptist deacon who personally MOTIONED the 2020 2A Constitutional resolution AND consistently pushed for deeper tax cuts reads as moderate-conservative-pragmatist on the cells where he has spoken on the record. NOTE: only 4 of 30 cells affirmatively scored (1 economic, 2 refuse_state_overreach, 1 self_defense — all TRUE); the rest are honestly null pending Suffolk Agenda Center primary-records review. The 4-TRUE/0-FALSE on a 4-cell denominator yields 100% of scored cells but the headline dynamic-max-denominator math means this scores 13/100 on the 30-cell tier basis — accurately reflecting strong-but-narrow evidence. Recommend revisiting after Suffolk Agenda Center minutes parse and any 2026 Pride / sanctuary / abortion / CRT-SOGI council action.
